name: schema-forward-compat
description: Two `overlay.set` events on the same wave: (a) a v1-shaped status overlay with NO schemaVersion field (the only shape any pre-#198 row has ever had), (b) the same status overlay rewritten by a hypothetical future kernel at schemaVersion=999. Post-#220 every client-facing read surface filters (b): WS `/api/events` replay drops the v999 row via `should_skip_event_for_overlay_version` (advancing `last_id` past it), and `GET /api/overlays` drops it via `filter_unsupported_overlay_versions`. So this fixture pins the symmetric forward-compat contract — both reads agree, frontend cannot observe v999. The accompanying test sanity-checks `payload_schema_version()` reads `1` for (a) and `999` for (b), then drives both client-facing reads against a seeded log to confirm only (a) surfaces. Regenerating from a real session must keep both shapes round-trippable through `seed_events → WS replay (filter applied) → loader`.
